novel_features.name: Most-visited resorts (multi-season)
novel_features.description: Rank the resorts you ski most across every season you've held an Ikon Pass, with per-season day counts.
novel_features.rationale: The API exposes usage only per single-season pass id, and those ids rotate yearly; only a tool that enumerates all your passes and joins their redemption histories can show lifetime totals.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user asks where they ski most, how many days they've used over the years, or for a season-by-season breakdown.

novel_features.name: Cross-resort trip planner
novel_features.description: Across every reservation-required resort, show which have your target dates open — one call instead of checking four resorts by hand.
novel_features.rationale: Requires fanning out the per-resort availability endpoint and joining the results against a date window locally; no single upstream call does this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when the user wants to ski a date range and doesn't care which of the reservable mountains it is.

novel_features.name: Availability calendar
novel_features.description: Month grid of open vs full vs blackout vs closed days for a reservable resort.
novel_features.rationale: The API returns flat date arrays; rendering them as a navigable month view is local presentation the upstream UI gates behind login clicks.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to eyeball a whole month of reservation openings for one resort at a glance.

novel_features.name: Reservation watch
novel_features.description: Poll a resort+date and alert the moment a currently-full day frees up.
novel_features.rationale: Requires repeated local polling and diffing of the availability endpoint against a target date — stateful behavior no single API call provides.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a desired reservation date is full and the user wants to grab it if someone cancels.

novel_features.name: Availability drift
novel_features.description: After each sync, report which dates opened or closed at your watched resorts since the last snapshot.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local SQLite history of availability snapshots to diff against; the API is stateless and never reports change-over-time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to see what reservation availability shifted day-over-day without re-reading every resort manually.

narrative.headline: Ikon Pass CLI for reservation availability, pass and voucher lookup, multi-season usage history, and offline change tracking.
narrative.value_prop: Check reservation availability at the resorts that require it, see your passes and Friends & Family vouchers, and rank the mountains you've skied most across every season you've held a pass. Reads the same private API the Ikon account site uses, with a local SQLite mirror so history and change-tracking work offline.
narrative.auth_narrative: Ikon uses cookie-session auth minted through Auth0 SSO — there's no API key. The public `resorts` command needs nothing. Everything tied to your account (availability, passes, vouchers, usage) reads your `.ikonpass.com` session cookie: run `ikon-pp-cli auth login --chrome` to import it from Chrome, or install the `press-auth` companion for a controlled one-window capture that survives session expiry. Sessions last roughly a day; re-import when commands start returning 401.

quickstart.comment: confirm the API is reachable — no login needed
browse every Ikon destination and its numeric id
reservation availability for Jackson Hole (id 14) once you're logged in
rank the resorts you've skied most across all your seasons

troubleshoots.fix: Your session expired or wasn't imported — run `ikon-pp-cli auth login --chrome` (or `press-auth login ikonpass.com`).

troubleshoots.symptom: 'must be authenticated' even right after logging in
troubleshoots.fix: Ikon keeps the session cookie where the basic reader can't see it; install the press-auth companion: 'go install github.com/mvanhorn/cli-printing-press/v4/cmd/press-auth@latest' then 'press-auth login ikonpass.com --login-url https://account.ikonpass.com/login'.
The default browser-like User-Agent clears Incapsula; if you overrode it, restore a normal Chrome UA.
Expected off-season — usage populates once the season's redemptions begin; past seasons appear immediately if their passes are on your account.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI for anything tied to an Ikon Pass account: checking whether a reservation-required resort (Deer Valley, Jackson Hole, Loon, Snoqualmie) has open days, reviewing your passes and Friends & Family voucher balance, and analyzing your ski history across seasons. It is read-only and offline-first — it mirrors the account API into local SQLite so history, calendars, and change-tracking work without re-fetching.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to BOOK, modify, or cancel reservations — it is read-only by design.
Do not use it to share or send Friends & Family vouchers — it reports remaining/used counts only.
Do not use it for resorts that don't require reservations — most Ikon destinations are walk-on and won't appear in availability.
Do not use it to buy or renew a pass — purchasing happens on ikonpass.com.

recipes.title: Lifetime resort ranking for an agent
recipes.explanation: Returns season-joined day counts per resort as JSON an agent can summarize directly.

recipes.title: Trip planning across reservable resorts
recipes.explanation: Shows which of the reservation-required resorts have open days in the window.

recipes.title: Narrow resort data with field selection
recipes.explanation: The resorts payload is large; --select trims it to just the fields an agent needs to resolve a name to an id.

recipes.title: Watch a full date for openings
recipes.explanation: Polls Jackson Hole for 2026-01-17 and alerts when the date frees up.

trigger_phrases: is jackson hole open for reservations
how many days have I skied this season
what resorts do I ski most
ikon friends and family vouchers left
